title: Preparation of midbrain slices for electrophysiology
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9btee","text":"Protocol for the preparation of midbrain slices for electrophysiology","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3qe7c","text":"This procedure was carried out following the guidelines on the ethical use of animals from the Council Directive of the European Communities (2010/63/EU) and was approved by the Italian Ministry of Health","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Anesthetize the rat with isoflurane and decapitate it.

Dissect the brain and immerse it in a low-sodium N-methyl-D-glucamine (NMDG)-based artificial cerebrospinal fluid (aCSF) at 2-4 °C. The NMDG-based solution contains (in mM): 92 NMDG, 2.5 KCl, 1.25 NaH 2 PO 4 , 30 NaHCO 3 , 20 HEPES, 25 glucose, 2 thiourea, 5 Na-ascorbate, 3 Na-pyruvate, 0.5 CaCl 2 , and 10 MgSO 4 , saturated with 95% O 2 –5% CO 2 (pH 7.3)
Cut the brain with a vibratome to obtain horizontal slices (250 μm) including the SNpc
Maintain the slices in NMDG-based aCSF at 33.0 ± 0.5°C for 15 min before adding increasing volumesof a sodium-spike solution (2M NaCl in NMDG-based aCSF) every 5 min for 40 min

Transfer the slices in a HEPES-based aCSF for long-term storage, containing (in mM): 92 NaCl, 2.5 KCl, 1.25 NaH 2 PO 4 , 30 NaHCO 3 , 20 HEPES, 25 glucose, 2 thiourea, 5 Na-ascorbate, 3 Na-pyruvate, 2 CaCl 2 , and 2 MgSO 4 (pH 7.3)

After 1h, transfer the slices in the recording chamber and perfuse at 2.5–3.0 mL/min with aCSF (33.0 ± 0.5°C) containing (in mM): NaCl 126, NaHCO 3 24, glucose 10, KCl 2.5, CaCl 2 2.4, NaH 2 PO 4 1.2 and MgCl 2 1.2 (95% O 2 –5% CO 2 , pH 7.3).
creator: Ada Ledonne
